text:
Once there was a wise old owl named Olga. She owned a cozy little nest by the riverbank. People from all around the forest would visit her to hear her wise tales. One summer day, a little mouse named Marcus arrived.

"Why such a strange nest?" the mouse asked.

"Sometimes," Olga replied, "I like my nest better."

Marcus nibbled on an acorn. "What kind of wisdom do you have today?"

"Oh," Olga began, her eyes twinkling. "About love."

Marcus leaned closer. "Tell me."

"I've never known anyone truly unhappy because they didn't love someone..."

"Why?" Marcus interrupted.

"Because you can't live forever," Olga sighed.

"The only thing I'd ever want is love."

Olga tilted her head. "And what would that be?"

"A wise old owl."

They became friends. One foggy morning, Olga sang them a song, about a wise old owl who found happiness in the forest.
